topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to bleed into my vision. I stared at the 'D-' with a detached horror, my hands trembling slightly as I picked up the graded essay. Every word I had poured onto that page, every late night fueled by lukewarm coffee, felt utterly invalidated. A heavy, dull ache settled in my chest, a physical weight pressing down on my lungs. I traced the poorly marked sections with a fingertip, the texture of the paper rough against my skin. When I finally shoved the paper into my backpack, the sound of the zipper closing felt deafeningly final. I walked home in a daze, the usual vibrancy of the afternoon drained from the world, leaving only a flat, gray expanse.

[story 2]

My stomach clenched into a tight knot as I read the notification on my screen. The number flashed, a stark, unforgiving score that mocked the hours I’d invested. I let out a slow, ragged breath, the air catching painfully in my throat. I sat at my desk, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead, and found myself just staring at the blank wall, unable to summon the energy to even look at my notes. My shoulders slumped forward, a complete surrender to the disappointment. "This is it," I whispered to the empty room, the sound barely audible. The silence that followed was thick, suffocating, and utterly isolating.

[story 3]

I kept re-reading the feedback, my eyes skipping over the critiques as if they were meaningless noise. Each sentence felt like a tiny, sharp jab. A strange, hot flush crept up my neck, and I instinctively pulled my sweater tighter around myself, a futile attempt to ward off some internal chill. My jaw felt clenched so hard it ached. I started pacing the small confines of my room, the floorboards creaking under my hurried steps, needing to move, needing to escape the confines of that disappointing result. When I stopped by the window, I watched the world outside move with an effortless grace that I felt entirely disconnected from.
